g.title: directionality_decision_diagram
g.text: Empirical Flow Orientation on Divided-Road Study Rows
For one segmented one-side roadway row at a time, is there enough conservative crash evidence to assign local travel direction, or should the row stay unresolved?

g.title: input_fields
g.text: attached crashes + crash direction of travel

g.title: rule_terms
g.text: this is the row that receives the local flow label

g.title: rule_qualifying
g.text: exclude ambiguous, compound, or multi-vehicle reads

g.title: rule_thresholds
g.text: prefer unanimity, but allow a strong majority

g.title: dominant
g.text: If not, is there still a strong majority

g.title: strict_outcome
g.text: so local flow is assigned conservatively

g.title: empirical90_outcome
g.text: one direction holds a strong majority,
so local flow is assigned with a bounded relaxation

g.title: out_main
g.text: Unresolved = evidence too thin or still conflicting

g.title: out_review
g.text: but does not replace crash evidence

g.title: out_handoff
g.text: this becomes row-level directed flow support
